issues.title: Add a builder for user resource-sharing request bodies (build_user_rs_request)
issues.body_raw:
**Complexity:** S
**Benefit:** High

## Problem

`create_user_rs_request` forwards `request_data` **verbatim**, so every caller must
hand-assemble the body and get the plain-vs-`{"value": ...}` wrapping right. This
was the direct cause of a long trial-and-error loop while building a borrowing
request:

- `owner` must be a **plain string** (not wrapped) — a well-known Alma quirk.
- `format`, `citation_type`, `pickup_location` must be **wrapped** `{"value": ...}`.
- `pickup_location_type` is a **plain string**.
- Wrong shape or wrong code → cryptic Alma errors (e.g. `Invalid field value …
  Value: {1}`).

## Proposed

A helper that builds the correctly-shaped body from plain keyword arguments:

```python
build_user_rs_request(
    owner="RES_SHARE",
    format="DIGITAL",
    citation_type="E_CR",
    title=..., journal_title=..., author=..., year=...,
    pickup_location="RES_SHARE", pickup_location_type="LIBRARY",
    agree_to_copyright_terms=True,
    external_id=...,          # see idempotency issue
    extra=None,               # escape hatch for advanced fields
)
```

It encodes the wrapping rules (and the `owner`-plain quirk) exactly once. Keep the
raw-dict path available for advanced callers.

## Related

Pairs with opt-in value validation (#194) and the idempotency/reconcile work.



## Files to touch

- `src/almaapitk/domains/users.py`
- `src/almaapitk/__init__.py`
- `tests/unit/domains/test_users.py`
- `tests/unit/regressions/test_issue_197.py`

issues.title: create_user_rs_request: no guardrail against wrong code-table values → cryptic Alma error
issues.body_raw:
**Complexity:** S
**Benefit:** Medium

## Summary

`Users.create_user_rs_request()` forwards `request_data` to Alma **verbatim** (by
design — see its docstring) and performs no validation of code-table field
*values*. When a caller sends a value from the **wrong Alma code table** — e.g.
`format: {"value": "P"}` (a *purchase-request* format code) on the **borrowing**
endpoint, which expects `PHYSICAL` / `DIGITAL` — Alma rejects it with a mangled,
un-actionable error that does **not name the offending field**:

```
Invalid field value. Field: [Ljava.lang.Object;@2f3cde3, Value: {1}
```

(`[Ljava.lang.Object;@…` is a Java array's `toString()`; `{1}` is an
unsubstituted message-template placeholder — Alma itself fails to render which
field is bad. The developer gets no signal about which field or why.)

## Reproduction

Payload identical to the passing regression-smoke test
(`chunks/users-requests-followup/sandbox-tests/test_t_42_3.py`) **except**
`format`:

```python
users.create_user_rs_request(user_id, {
    "owner": "<RS_LIB>",
    "format": {"value": "P"},            # ← wrong table (purchase P/E); borrowing wants PHYSICAL/DIGITAL
    "citation_type": {"value": "BOOK"},
    "title": "…",
    "pickup_location_type": "LIBRARY",
    "pickup_location": {"value": "<LIB>"},
    "agree_to_copyright_terms": True,
})
# -> AlmaAPIError: Invalid field value. Field: [Ljava.lang.Object;@…, Value: {1}
```

Changing `format` to `{"value": "PHYSICAL"}` makes the same call succeed.

## Why this is easy to hit

Three RS-adjacent surfaces use **different code values for the same conceptual
field**, and the docs partly disagree with each other:

| Field | Purchase request | Lending request | **Borrowing (this endpoint)** |
|---|---|---|---|
| `format` | `P` / `E` | `PHYSICAL` / `DIGITAL` | `PHYSICAL` / `DIGITAL` |
| `citation_type` | `BOOK` / `JOURNAL` | `BOOK` / `JOURNAL` | `BOOK` (empirical, per `test_t_42_3.py`) — but `docs/Alma Borrowing Request API Guide.md` lists the XSD codes `BK` / `CR` |

A caller who reused a `format` code from the purchase or an older skill/reference
gets the cryptic error above with nothing pointing at `format`.

## Suggestions (non-prescriptive — code tables are tenant-extensible)

A closed hardcoded allowlist would be brittle (institutions can extend code
tables). Options, in increasing invasiveness:

1. **Better error surfacing (low-risk, no behavior change to valid calls).**
   In the response error handler, detect the
   `Invalid field value … [Ljava.lang.Object; … {1}` shape and raise
   `AlmaAPIError` with an appended hint, e.g. *"a code-table field value was
   rejected; on the RS-borrowing surface `format` expects PHYSICAL/DIGITAL and
   `citation_type` expects BOOK/CR"*. Purely additive.

2. **Optional soft validation.** Validate the well-known borrowing code-table
   fields (`format`, `citation_type`, `pickup_location_type`) against documented
   defaults *before* the network call, raising `AlmaValidationError` naming the
   field — gated behind a `validate=True` (or `strict=`) kwarg so tenants with
   custom codes can opt out.

3. **Docs / builder helper.** Document a known-good borrowing payload plus a
   purchase-vs-lending-vs-borrowing code cheat sheet in the docstring / API
   reference (partly exists in `docs/Alma Borrowing Request API Guide.md`, which
   itself disagrees with the passing test on `citation_type`), and/or ship a
   `build_user_rs_request_data(...)` helper that emits the correctly-wrapped body
   so callers don't hand-roll wrapping + codes.

Even option 1 alone would turn a dead-end error into an actionable one.

## Environment

- `almaapitk` 0.4.5 (source tree; `create_user_rs_request` present)
- Endpoint: `POST /almaws/v1/users/{user_id}/resource-sharing-requests`
- Observed in SANDBOX, 2026-07-19.



## Files to touch

- `src/almaapitk/domains/users.py`
- `src/almaapitk/client/AlmaAPIClient.py`
- `tests/unit/domains/test_users.py`
- `tests/unit/regressions/test_issue_194.py`
